calls.summary: Deep-discovery close. Customer (70s, post-cardiac surgery) lost 15–20 yards across all irons and had chronic outside-in slice. Rep answered shipping question first to build trust, then ran full 38-minute discovery. Closed on PG-One All Access at $29/month. Customer score goal: shoot his age.
strengths: Led with trust-building by answering shipping inquiry before pitching
Deep patient discovery — 38-minute call with full game profile
Connected product directly to slice elimination and distance recovery
Used aspirational goal anchor ('shoot my age') effectively
opportunities: Verbal card collection created compliance gap when secure link failed
Group referral opportunity missed — customer plays Kiva Dunes with 25–35 friends annually
VIP coaching not explored
objections: Self-doubt about never having taken a formal lesson
Hesitation about investment at his age
Worried straight driver would disrupt intentional slice on dogleg holes
recommendations: Review verbal card collection compliance process
Brief PG-One advisor with full customer profile before coaching session
Use as positive training example for closer team on discovery depth

calls.summary: Strong rebound call from Julius (who had 7 declines on June 24). Mark Roberts, 65, shoots 90s with slice and chipping issues. Full discovery executed. Root-flaw language deployed. Identity close used. Booked Friday 5PM ET Zoom despite prospect flagging a 9AM appointment at the 10-minute mark.
strengths: Discovery-first sequencing — ran full game assessment before revealing program
Root-flaw framing resonated with prospect's 'throwing darts at it' frustration
Identity close ('which one are you?') generated self-commitment
Handled time constraint efficiently — compressed to booking close
opportunities: Price pre-emption block still needed if harder objections arise
Advisor handoff profile not yet documented
objections: Time constraint: 9AM appointment flagged at 10-minute mark
Commitment uncertainty: 'I don't know how big a part of my life I want golf to be'
Past lesson attempts hadn't produced results
recommendations: Share as positive training example — contrast with June 24 performance
Continue coaching Julius on price pre-emption and harder objections
Brief Zoom advisor with full discovery profile before Friday call

calls.summary: Clean outbound post-purchase follow-up. Customer asked 'What's the chat about?' — handled with benefit-focused response. Booked 4:00 PM ET advisor slot with multiple options offered. Continues June 24 pattern: zero discovery questions, no VIP coaching offer.
strengths: Professional execution — all scheduling details confirmed cleanly
Handled clarifying question with benefit-focused, non-defensive answer
Multiple time slots offered (4:00 PM, 6:20 PM, 6:40 PM, 7:00 PM ET)
opportunities: Zero discovery questions asked — 4-question framework not used
No VIP coaching offer — missed upsell opportunity
Advisor receives cold handoff with no customer context
objections: 'What's the chat about?' — curiosity, not resistance
recommendations: Escalate script update — discovery + VIP offer recommendations overdue from June 24
Add mandatory golf context question before confirming slot

calls.summary: Inbound order — Roy buying 357 ladies flex as Father's Day gift for wife. Call extended to 30 minutes due to payment portal card entry failures (expiration date field broken). Customer explicitly planned to cancel PG-One trial before being charged. Order completed at $186.75 + tax using AmEx as backup card.
strengths: Exceptional patience throughout 30-minute payment portal troubleshooting
Clear billing transparency — explained $29/month post-trial charge explicitly
Order completed successfully despite significant technical friction
opportunities: Payment portal expiration date field bug caused 15+ extra minutes — urgent tech ticket needed
Website checkout does not clearly disclose $29/month PG-One recurring charge
Customer pre-planned to cancel trial — billing disclosure gap enabling trial exploits
objections: Auto-billing concern: 'A lot of times you do this stuff and you forget about it'
Marketing opt-out: didn't want texts or emails
Checkout page didn't disclose ongoing charges
recommendations: File urgent tech ticket for payment portal expiration date field bug
Update checkout page to prominently disclose $29/month after trial
Explore gift-purchase PG-One opt-out at checkout

calls.summary: Outbound retention follow-up. Customer had already spoken to 2 reps about this account and demanded this contact stop. 'Please stop calling me every day.' Firm DNC request. Call should not have been placed given prior contact history in CRM.
strengths: Call ended without escalation
opportunities: CRM call frequency controls absent — customer called despite 2 prior contacts
No DNC flag triggered after previous contacts
objections: 'I've had this conversation with two people. Please stop calling me every day.'
recommendations: Flag account as DNC immediately
Implement CRM rule: 2+ outbound touches in 7 days requires manager approval before next call

calls.summary: Outbound retention call. Customer purchased a club, it didn't work for him, and he's returning it. No interest in any program. Professional, clean handling of an unwinnable call.
strengths: Clean professional close — no pressure applied
Acknowledged the return gracefully
Call ended on positive note despite no save
opportunities: No discovery attempted to understand why club didn't work
No alternative product offered
objections: 'The only reason I got it was to try the club, and the club just didn't work for me, so I'm sending it back.'
recommendations: Add a brief product diagnostic question before accepting return — may reveal a fit/instruction issue that's solvable

calls.summary: Complex VIP coaching billing dispute. John Bautista was charged $99/month for 5 months (Feb–Jun) — $495 total — without knowingly enrolling. Claims he never agreed to VIP coaching. Nonie ran exceptional de-escalation: empathy, account review, value re-anchor. Customer insisted on refund-first before re-engaging. Full $495 refund committed. Customer expressed willingness to reconsider after resolution.
strengths: Exceptional de-escalation on a complex $495 billing dispute
Led with empathy and account review rather than defense
Preserved the relationship — customer expressed willingness to re-engage post-refund
Correctly respected customer's 'refund first' boundary
opportunities: Enrollment audit needed — how many others were enrolled in VIP coaching without explicit consent?
No pause or trial reset option was available as an alternative to full refund
VIP coaching trial-to-paid conversion should require explicit double-opt-in
objections: 'I never verbally or through an email or app signed up for anything'
'I want all 5 months credited first, then I'll call back when I want the trial to start'
'The approach — charging without my agreement — is not fair'
recommendations: Conduct enrollment audit of Dec 4th email-conversion pipeline
Add explicit consent checkpoint to VIP coaching enrollment flow
Implement pause/freeze option as save tool between discount and full refund

calls.summary: PG-One annual cancellation. Jeff had login issues from day one and only reached the driving range content once. Plays golf 2–3 times per year. Declined $50 off (→$249) and $100 off (→$199) offers. Refunded. The real objection was low play frequency and behavioral mismatch — price discounting was the wrong tool.
strengths: Empathetic handling — no pressure
Discount offers were genuine attempts to save
Clean close on a call with no viable save path given the constraints
opportunities: Price discount misdiagnosed a behavioral objection (low usage, low play frequency)
Pause option would have been more appropriate than two failed price cuts
Login issues from day one were never resolved — a failure of onboarding
objections: 'I've never been able to really log into the account anyways — from day one I had issues'
'I maybe play golf 2 to 3 times a year. The membership isn't really for me.'
Declined $50 and $100 discount offers — real issue was play frequency, not price
recommendations: Add play frequency qualifier to trial enrollment — route low-frequency golfers to a different tier
Introduce pause option as save tool before discount offers
Add proactive login troubleshooting to post-purchase onboarding flow (Days 1–3)

calls.summary: Inbound billing concern — $322 charge. Omar immediately stopped all future subscriptions, clarified what Mary had already paid for (full year training platform + GPS access), and sent email confirmation. Customer satisfied. Marked Saved because charges stopped and customer retained year-long access, though no golf context was established.
strengths: Efficient non-defensive resolution in under 3 minutes
Stopped future charges immediately — addressed the core concern first
Sent email confirmation — professional follow-through
opportunities: No discovery — customer's actual golf situation unknown
No retention pitch beyond stopping charges
No engagement with the product's value before closing
objections: 'I do not want that, and I don't want to be charged that. I've already paid a bunch of money.'
recommendations: Add one golf context question before closing the save — 'Have you had a chance to use the platform at all?'

calls.summary: Outbound post-purchase call. John (70s, arthritis) purchased RS-ONE Putter and loved it, but flagged a design flaw: the flange prevents ball pickup — a safety risk. He self-modified the club. Daniel acknowledged the feedback, requested photo documentation, then offered a 30-day masterclass trial (extended from standard 7 days as goodwill) and closed at $299 annually.
strengths: Handled product complaint professionally — listened without defensiveness, took ownership
Extended trial from 7 to 30 days as loyalty gesture — landed well
Read the room on VIP coaching — correctly pivoted away when customer mentioned his local pro
Discovery executed: game level, medical context, product preferences all surfaced
opportunities: RS-ONE Putter ball-pickup design complaint needs escalation to product/engineering team
Customer offered to send photo of self-modification — engineering feedback loop opportunity
objections: RS-ONE flange design prevents safe ball pickup — customer had to modify the club
Declined wedge pitch: 'I love my wedges and there's no freaking way I'm changing'
Has existing pro relationship — no VIP coaching interest
recommendations: Escalate RS-ONE Putter ball-pickup design complaint to product/engineering immediately
Flag John Caravolas as potential brand advocate — vocal, positive, and has already influenced his son

calls.summary: Top-scoring ASR call on June 24. Clean execution: purchase confirmation, PG-One explanation, time zone check, slot options, advisor credentialing — all completed. Highest QA score on the team (8.5/10). Continued pattern: zero discovery questions, no VIP coaching offer.
strengths: Highest QA score on Team Lee: 8.5/10
Efficient and professional execution
All appointment details confirmed cleanly
opportunities: Zero discovery questions — 4-question framework skipped
VIP coaching offer not made
recommendations: Add discovery questions to ASR post-purchase script before confirming slot

calls.summary: Post-purchase follow-up for Curt Bousum (SF-One Driver). Booking made for PG-One advisor consultation. Customer flagged a billing concern during checkout — Gabriella verified and committed to shipping follow-up. This is the same customer later closed by Emmanuel Sandoval on June 25 (PG-One All Access). ASR → Closer pipeline working as intended.
strengths: Real-time order verification — confirmed billing concern and committed to follow-up
Smooth handoff to the advisor pipeline
opportunities: Zero discovery — advisor and closer received no game context for Curt
objections: Checkout billing concern — possible confusion about promotional items
recommendations: Pass game context forward to advisor and closer when it's captured in ASR call

calls.summary: Best rapport of the ASR team on June 24. Warm humor, natural conversational cadence. Mentioned advisor's name, Fort Lauderdale origin, and encouraged prospect to ask lots of questions. Precise appointment detail confirmation.
strengths: Best rapport of Team Lee — warm humor, natural cadence
Advisor credentialed with name + expertise framing
Precise appointment details confirmed
opportunities: Zero discovery questions asked
VIP coaching offer not made
recommendations: Use as tonality model for other ASR reps — especially Carlo Palma

calls.summary: Lowest-scoring ASR call on June 24 (6/10). Flat delivery, zero enthusiasm, zero discovery, zero VIP offer. Appointment booked but experience was transactional rather than consultative. Customer engagement was minimal.
strengths: Appointment confirmed successfully
opportunities: Flat, transactional tone throughout — needs enthusiasm coaching
Zero discovery questions
Zero VIP offer
No rapport-building attempts
recommendations: Prioritize Carlo Palma for targeted tonality and enthusiasm coaching
Use Marwin Ray Reyes call as contrast example in coaching session

calls.summary: Emmanuella Frago scored consistently 7–7.5/10 on June 24. Strong on scheduling objections — offered multiple slots and handled time constraints well. Weak on discovery. No VIP coaching offer made.
strengths: Handled scheduling objections well — offered flexible slots
Warm professional tone
opportunities: Discovery questions not asked
VIP coaching offer not made
objections: Time availability concern — working/busy schedule
recommendations: Good foundation — coaching should focus on adding 1–2 discovery questions before the booking confirmation

calls.summary: Kim Paolo Planas scored 7/10. Standard post-purchase follow-up with clean execution. No discovery, no VIP offer. Appointment details confirmed.
strengths: Clean professional execution
Appointment details confirmed
opportunities: No discovery questions
No VIP coaching offer
recommendations: Apply script update once published — same gaps as rest of Team Lee

calls.summary: Best-practice Internal Setter call on June 24. Phoebe ran the full script: warm open, full discovery, root-flaw framing, 'which one are you?' identity close, two-slot time close. Booked Saturday Zoom. Used '9,000 golfers' social proof and customization language to deflect price question before running discovery.
strengths: Full script executed correctly — all phases completed
Price deflection: 'cost varies person to person — I need to make sure it's a good fit first'
'Which one are you?' identity close deployed effectively
Root-flaw language: 'we don't just fix the miss — we find the root cause'
Social proof: 9,000 golfers + top-10 coaches
opportunities: Minor: score-goal anchor could have been made more explicit
objections: Initial hesitation about time commitment
recommendations: Use as gold-standard training example for the Internal Setter team

calls.summary: Dan Emmanuel Nicolas was the top-performing Internal Setter on June 24 (7 bookings / 3 declines). This call shows his consistent pattern: discovery-first, root-flaw framing, score goal anchoring, clean time close. Highest conversion efficiency on the team.
strengths: Highest booking efficiency on the team (7:3 booked-to-declined ratio)
Discovery-first sequencing — never revealed price before running full game assessment
Score goal anchor made the investment feel purposeful
Confident, warm execution throughout
opportunities: No specific gaps identified — call followed best-practice script
objections: Initial 'I'm not sure I have time' — reframed effectively
recommendations: Use as a peer coaching resource — pair with lower-performing reps

calls.summary: Second Dan Emmanuel Nicolas booking on June 24. Consistent execution pattern: full discovery, root-flaw framing, identity close. Prospect was initially hesitant but converted after score goal framing.
strengths: Consistent replication of full script across multiple calls
Score goal anchor used to move hesitant prospect to commitment
objections: Hesitant about the program's time requirements
recommendations: Document Dan's approach as a repeatable playbook for the team

calls.summary: Glenn Huth asked about price in the opening seconds: 'I'd rather just cut to the chase — if it's a price point I can't afford, there's no sense.' Julius answered the price question immediately instead of deflecting. Price revealed cold ($3,000–$4,000). Call declined within 2 minutes. Julius's most common pattern on June 24.
opportunities: Price revealed without discovery — no value established before the number landed
No script-based deflection attempted: 'I want to make sure the number I give you is relevant to your specific game'
Call was over in 3 minutes — price shock exited the prospect immediately
objections: 'I'd rather just cut to the chase — if it's a price I can't afford, there's no sense going through my game.'
'Wow, okay, yeah.' — price revealed cold, call ended within seconds
recommendations: Julius needs price pre-emption coaching urgently
Add confidence and script adherence to Julius's 1:1 coaching plan

calls.summary: Richard Alvarez opened with: 'I was really just fishing to see what the price point is.' Julius engaged with price conversation directly instead of deflecting. Same pattern as Glenn Huth call. Second cost-decline for Julius on June 24 using the same approach.
opportunities: Same script failure as previous call — price pre-emption block not deployed
Repeat of the same avoidable loss pattern
objections: 'I was really just fishing to see what the price point is.' — stated in opening
recommendations: Immediate 1:1 coaching session — Julius needs the price deflection block before next shift

calls.summary: Cathy York declined due to location: 'I'm not going to Florida or Texas or California. I want something right here in St. Louis, Missouri.' Hard-stop location objection. Heinrich had no local-option response prepared. Nationally distributed prospect base with a U.S.-only limited-location academy creates a structural DQ problem.
strengths: Call handled professionally — no argument made
opportunities: No location qualifier asked in first 2 minutes — 3-minute call ended at the reveal
No local option to offer — structural gap in the program's footprint vs. prospect geography
objections: 'I'm not going to Florida or Texas or California to take a golf lesson. I want something right here in St. Louis.'
recommendations: Add state/location qualifier to first 2 minutes of setter script
Develop a local-alternative option or online-only offer for location-DQ'd prospects

calls.summary: C. Randolph Keller declined due to professional schedule: 'I can't take 5 days and go to Florida to a golf academy. I'm not in a position to do that because of demands in my profession.' Time and location combined DQ. Heinrich had no flexible schedule option to offer.
strengths: Professional handling of an unwinnable objection combo
opportunities: No multi-day schedule flexibility offered
No online/hybrid program alternative presented
objections: 'I can't take 5 days off from professional demands to attend a multi-day academy away from home'
recommendations: Develop a short-format (1–2 day) or virtual option for time-poor professional prospects

calls.summary: Scratch Club membership cancellation processed by Internal Setter team. Customer called to cancel. Rep complied with zero retention attempt, zero save pitch. Score 3.5/10. This call should have been handled with a save script — no attempt was made.
strengths: Cancel processed efficiently
opportunities: Zero retention attempt — no save script used
Internal Setter team is processing Scratch Club cancellations without save protocol
No empathy sequence, no pause option offered, no feature re-anchor
objections: 'I didn't even know that I was a member. I'd like to cancel my membership.'
recommendations: Build Scratch Club save micro-script for setter reps handling inbound cancellations
Flag these calls for retention team routing instead

calls.summary: Second Scratch Club cancellation processed by Internal Setter team on June 24. Louise Finch called about a monthly billing charge she didn't recognize. Rep cancelled without retention attempt. Score 3.8/10. Even Dan Emmanuel Nicolas — the top booking performer — had no save protocol for this call type.
strengths: Friendly, efficient handling
opportunities: Zero retention attempt by even the team's top closer
Scratch Club save protocol completely absent from Internal Setter team
objections: 'I'm getting a bill every month, so I would like to cancel the one I'm getting charged for.'
recommendations: Implement Scratch Club save protocol across all teams that handle inbound cancellations
